title: Medroxyprogesterone Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: r medications, foods or substances. Tell your doctor or pharmacist about the allergy and what symptoms you had. tell your doctor and pharmacist what prescription and nonprescription medications, vitamins, nutritional supplements, and herbal products you are taking or plan to take while using medroxyprogesterone injection. Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. the following herbal product may interact with medroxyprogesterone injection: St. John's wort. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start using medroxyprogesterone injection. Do not start any of these medications while using medroxyprogesterone injection without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you or anyone in your family has or has ever had breast cancer or diabetes. Also tell your doctor if you have or have ever had problems with your breasts such as lumps, bleeding from your nipples, an abnormal mammogram (breast x-ray), or fibrocystic breast disease (swollen, tender breasts and/or breast lumps that are not cancer); unexplained vaginal bleeding; irregular or very light menstrual periods; excessive weight gain or fluid retention before your period; blood clots in your legs, lungs, brain, or eyes; stroke or mini-stroke; migraine headaches; seizures; depression; high blo
